summary: Full frontend UI testing of the Excalidraw-like Whiteboard app. All 21 critical flows (panel rendering, 10 tools, drawing rect/pen/line/arrow/text, undo/redo, theme, save/export JSON/PNG, zoom, selection+delete, properties panel, keyboard shortcuts, new-scene) tested and verified working. No backend (frontend-only app).
critical_code_review_comments: Whiteboard.jsx is 1454 lines — recommend splitting into modules (e.g., TopBar.jsx, LeftToolbar.jsx, PropertiesPanel.jsx, ZoomControls.jsx, hooks for history/drawing/storage). Currently one file holds rendering, drawing, history, theme, file I/O.
useState variable name 'props' shadows React's reserved word convention; consider renaming to 'styleProps' or 'shapeProps' for readability.

Continuous-erase in handlePointerMove (kind==='erase') calls pushHistory(next) once per element removed during a single drag, polluting the undo stack with many entries — should batch into a single history entry on pointerUp.
redraw() uses getComputedStyle(documentElement) for every frame to read CSS vars (--bg-canvas, --bg-grid, --accent). Cheap but unnecessary per-frame work; cache on theme change.

rough.canvas(canvas) is created once in resize layout-effect; this is fine but if canvas is resized on window resize, rough is not re-bound — currently OK because rough.canvas just stores a reference, but worth a comment.
Pen elements ignore roughness via getStroke (correct), but they still store the roughness prop unnecessarily.
exportPNG bakes the current --bg-canvas color into the PNG. The spec mentions transparent bg in the comment but code fills it. Decide & document explicitly.
Resize handles only support 4 corners (no edges) and pen/text resize is partial (text resize doesn't change rendered size, only stored width/height).
seed_data_creation: None — app starts with empty canvas / localStorage cleared before tests.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Whiteboard is frontend-only. To drive the text tool via Playwright, native page.mouse.click()/down+up sometimes does NOT trigger React's onPointerDown reliably on the canvas in this environment for the text branch (drawing rect/pen/line/arrow worked fine via mouse). A reliable workaround is to dispatch synthetic PointerEvents via page.evaluate (see iteration_1 scripts). All other interactions work normally. Storage key: 'e1-canvas-studio-scene-v1'. Theme key: 'e1-canvas-studio-theme'. Use page.on('dialog', accept) for the New-Scene confirm.
rca of the issue: No app bug. During automation, Playwright's page.mouse.click() at canvas center didn't reliably trigger the text editor in test runs while it worked for rect/pen/line/arrow drags. Dispatching a synthetic PointerEvent for pointerdown+pointerup correctly opens the canvas-text-input textarea, the user can type, Enter commits the text element, and scene-count increments. Real-user interaction is unaffected.
